name: routes
context: Configure Worker routes, custom domains, and zone bindings to map URLs to Workers
info: Worker routes define URL patterns that trigger a Worker. Routes are configured in `wrangler.toml` or via the API. A route pattern like `api.example.com/*` maps all requests to that path to the Worker. Custom domains provide automatic SSL and DNS — Cloudflare creates the DNS record and provisions the certificate. Zone routes require the domain to be on Cloudflare DNS. Routes support wildcards (`*`) for path matching. Multiple routes can point to the same Worker. Workers also get a default `*.workers.dev` subdomain.
triggers: add a route to a worker
point a domain to a worker
set up a custom domain for a worker
map a url to a worker
body:
# Worker Routes

Routes map URL patterns to Workers. There are three routing methods:

## 1. workers.dev Subdomain (Default)

Every Worker gets a free `<name>.<subdomain>.workers.dev` URL automatically.

```toml
# wrangler.toml
name = "my-api"
workers_dev = true  # default, enables *.workers.dev
```

URL: `https://my-api.<subdomain>.workers.dev`

## 2. Custom Domains (Recommended)

Custom domains provide automatic SSL and DNS configuration:

```toml
# wrangler.toml
routes = [
  { pattern = "api.example.com", custom_domain = true }
]
```

Cloudflare automatically:
- Creates a DNS record pointing to the Worker
- Provisions an SSL certificate
- Routes all traffic to the Worker

## 3. Zone Routes (Pattern Matching)

Zone routes use wildcard patterns to match URLs within a Cloudflare-managed zone:

```toml
# wrangler.toml
routes = [
  { pattern = "api.example.com/*", zone_name = "example.com" },
  { pattern = "example.com/api/*", zone_name = "example.com" },
]

# Or with zone_id instead of zone_name
routes = [
  { pattern = "api.example.com/*", zone_id = "zone-id-here" }
]
```

### Pattern Syntax

| Pattern | Matches |
|---------|----------|
| `api.example.com/*` | All paths under api.example.com |
| `*.example.com/*` | All subdomains and paths |
| `example.com/api/*` | Paths starting with /api/ |
| `api.example.com` | Exact subdomain (no path wildcard) |

## Route Priority

When multiple routes could match:
1. More specific hostname wins over wildcard
2. Longer path wins over shorter
3. Zone routes take priority over `workers.dev`

## API Management

```bash
# List routes for a zone
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/workers/routes" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"

# Create a route
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/workers/routes" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{"pattern":"api.example.com/*","script":"my-worker"}'

# Delete a route
curl -X DELETE "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$ZONE_ID/workers/routes/$ROUTE_ID" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN"
```

## Named Environments

```toml
# Production
routes = [{ pattern = "api.example.com/*", zone_name = "example.com" }]

# Staging
[env.staging]
routes = [{ pattern = "staging-api.example.com/*", zone_name = "example.com" }]
```

sections.title: Custom domains
sections.body: Set `custom_domain = true` in the route to have Cloudflare auto-configure DNS and SSL. This is the simplest way to connect a domain to a Worker. The domain must be in a zone on the same Cloudflare account. Custom domains handle all paths — no wildcard needed.

sections.title: Zone routes with patterns
sections.body: Zone routes use URL patterns with `*` wildcards. Specify `zone_name` or `zone_id` to target a Cloudflare zone. Patterns match against the full URL (hostname + path). The `*` wildcard matches any path segment. Multiple routes can be defined per Worker. Routes require the domain to have its DNS on Cloudflare.

sections.title: Route priority
sections.body: More specific routes win: exact hostname over wildcard, longer path over shorter. If a request matches routes for multiple Workers, the most specific match is used. Zone routes take priority over the default `workers.dev` subdomain.

related.note: DNS records must exist for zone routes to work

author: dojo-community
